channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Escape from Tarkov’s Player Base is Being Split Again
item.content:encoded:
In a post on social media, Nikita Buyanov, the head of the studio at Battlestate Games, confirmed that the player base is about to be split into a third category. Right now, you’ve got a clear separation between PvP and PvE players, but a third angle is about to come in, and it’ll see seasonal PMCs queue differently from ‘permanent’ PMCs.

Fans were quick to question the move, taking a mixed perspective on the revelation. For some, it makes complete sense that it would work this way to prevent ‘overpowered’ permanent players mixing with freshly-wiped PMCs, but for others, it’s spreading the players too thin and encouraging longer queue times.

PvP, PvE, and More PvP

Under a recent post from Nikita Buyanov, a question was asked by an Escape from Tarkov fan:

Nikita, will the seasonal PMCs be in the same queues as permanent server PMCs?

This is in reference to the imminent arrival of PMC characters that never face a wipe. Players are set to have seasonal characters that follow the traditional wipe pattern Escape from Tarkov has faced for many years. But during each season, special events, unlocks, and achievements will surface that you can only get on a seasonal character.

In a way, it aligns things with the PvE platform, which isn’t subjected to regular server-wide wipes. Those who were locked into their PvE characters only had to wipe during the 1.0 transition if they wanted to experience everything the supersized update had to offer.

Otherwise, PvE players just don’t wipe.

To the question above, Nikita simply answered, ‘No.’

One of the biggest Escape from Tarkov streamers in the business, LVNDMARK, wrote:

Honest question: do you think the game will have a large enough player base to support this?

Another long-time Tarkov fan and contributor to the ecosystem, LogicalSolutions, wrote:

Well… if current servers weren’t empty enough already, they’re definitely going to be extremely empty with seasonal characters being on different server. Was hoping this wouldn’t be the case.

Axel, another streamer important enough to have a ‘streamer item’ in Escape from Tarkov, tried to run some numbers. He pointed out that with a split between PvP, PvE, permanent PvP, ten maps, and daytime and nighttime rotations, the player base available is going to run very thin.

What do you think? Is this a good move? Let us know what you think on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Modern Warfare 2019 is blowing up again

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Strands #748 Hints and Answers for Today, March 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #748 for Saturday, March 21, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 21, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #748 are all related to the nose. So, think about what else you’d call your sniffer.

General Strands Hints For March 21

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Birds heave these
  • Hint 2: Car horns can be called this as well.
  • Hint 3: Dogs might be forced to wear these to muffle their barks.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #748 for March 21, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 19, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • SENT
  • ROBES
  • LETS
  • ROBE
  • MUZZ

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #748 On March 21, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SCHNOZZLE
  • HONKER
  • BEAK
  • SNOUT
  • MUZZLE
  • PROBOSCIS
  • SPANAGRAM: ONTHENOSE

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #748?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 22 will be Strands Game #749.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-748-hints-and-answers-for-today-march-21-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Palworld Devs Have No Desire to Be a Media Empire
item.content:encoded:
According to a top company executive, Pocketpair isn’t aiming to turn Palworld into a media empire.

Palworld is a successful open-world creature collector in the vein of Pokémon. The game has already spawned two titles: Palfarm, a cozy sim, and Palworld: More Than Just Pals, a school dating sim and visual novel. Despite these two games being in the works, Pocketpair has no desire to turn the series into a media empire-type franchise.

Pocketpair Wants to Make People Happy with Palworld Spinoffs

During an interview with GamesRadar+, Pocketpair’s head of publishing and communications, John Buckley, explained that he knows there is a market for all of the spinoffs in the game’s universe. They’re just trying to make people happy, but they don’t want to branch out too far.

“Personally, I don’t think Palworld is going to be like this media empire, and we have no desire to be a media empire. But I think we can expect more kind of fun little Palworld stuff. And maybe, you know, if someone wants to, maybe someone else can take a stab at it at some point.”

Buckley also recently discussed plans for Palworld’s 1.0 launch. See what’s in store for the game at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to provide any and all updates on Palworld-related news.

Have you previously heard of these spinoffs? Are you the kind of person who would play any of these games? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Minecraft World Opening in London in 2027
item.content:encoded:
Minecraft is bringing a theme park to London in 2027.

During Minecraft LIVE, it was announced that the franchise had been working with Merlin Entertainments to bring the world of Minecraft to life. The theme park will be part of London’s Chessington World of Adventures in 2027.

What to Expect from the Minecraft World Experience

It’s promised that the theme park will be packed with references to the game. The first-ever Minecraft roller coaster is in the works. There will also be food themed after the video game. There will be opportunities to meet the mobs and more.

“This is a truly epic collaboration between Mojang Studios and Merlin Entertainments, but we also recruited creators to help with some of the trickier challenges. We’ll share more on Minecraft World soon,” the team wrote in the recap blog.

Other Minecraft LIVE Announcements

Minecraft LIVE also announced the forthcoming sequel to Minecraft Dungeons. Additionally, the Sulfur Cube is the new mob coming to Minecraft. Learn more about the other major announcements from the stream at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to bring you the latest gaming news and rumors as they are made available. For more theme park news, check out news on a Resident Evil collaboration with Universal Studios Japan.

How do you feel about Minecraft World coming to London? Can you see a theme park like this landing in other spots around the world? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/minecraft-world-opening-in-london-in-2027/

item.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 Set for Release This Year
item.content:encoded:
Minecraft Dungeons 2 is slated for release in 2026.

The forthcoming game will be a follow-up to the May 2020 release. It’s slated for PC, PlayStation 5, and the Nintendo Switch family of consoles, the Xbox Series family of consoles, and Xbox Cloud Play.

A teaser trailer was revealed on March 21 during the Minecraft LIVE stream. The game is available to be wishlisted right now. There’s also an active Steam page with a game synopsis:

“A hero’s work is never done! Disorder is brewing, and the world stands before a threat unlike any other. A new danger is growing in power, ready to descend upon the land and unleash mayhem.

“Return to the world of Minecraft Dungeons in an all‑new action RPG adventure, brimming with high‑stakes encounters, thrilling challenges, and never‑before‑seen locations, as you set out to save a world in crisis. Will you and your allies prevail against the forces of evil and save the day once more?

Wishlist today to stay up to date on what’s coming next… “

Minecraft Dungeons Success

The original Minecraft Dungeons game was launched at the height of the global pandemic in May 2020. It has an average Metacritic score of 70 and a user score of 6.7. The game scored its highest on Metacritic on the PlayStation 4, with an overall score of 78.

Sulfur Cubes Added to Minecraft

Minecraft gameplay footage of the brand-new Sulfur Caves was shown. The development team also discusses what sets this area apart from others in the franchise. The game also features the new Sulfur Cube material, which can absorb other blocks, and features other ball-like physics that will add to this game’s longevity. For instance, Chaos Cubed is the new game coming to Minecraft

“This mob allows for even more creativity in your builds and refined chaos in your mini-games. We showcase a few ideas in this segment, but like everything we bring to the game, we’re mostly excited to see what you get up to when Chaos Cubed enters testing,” the team wrote.

How do you feel about Minecraft Dungeons? Are you excited for the sequel? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP
